title: Validating Git Branch Names with Non-ASCII Characters

statement.markdown: Write a function that validates whether a given Git branch name is valid. A valid Git branch name is one that matches the following regular expression: [a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9._-]{0,63}. However, this rule has an exception: when a branch name starts with a hyphen, it's only valid if the rest of the name is composed of alphanumeric characters. In this problem, you will need to write a function that checks whether a given branch name matches this rule. The function should return True for valid branch names and False for invalid branch names.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a string representing the Git branch name.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be a boolean indicating whether the branch name is valid.
statement.notes: You can assume that the input will only contain ASCII characters and will not be empty.

constraints.description: The length of the branch name will not exceed 10000 characters.

examples.explanation: The branch name 'main' is valid because it starts with a letter and only contains letters and digits.
The branch name '-main' is not valid because it starts with a hyphen but the rest of the name contains a non-alphanumeric character.
The branch name 'feature/new-feature' is valid because it starts with a letter and only contains letters, digits, dots, and hyphens.

hints.text: First hint text: Always check the first character of the branch name to see if it's a hyphen.
Second hint text: If the branch name starts with a hyphen, check the rest of the name to see if it only contains alphanumeric characters.
